title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Signal Triage
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
Select thread, memory, leak, race, or platform sanitizers according to risk and compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates sanitizers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Asan trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use addresssanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.

content.text: For Asan, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Asan, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-sanitizer-ci-18rjrc: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sanitizer Ci into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-sanitizer-ci-18rjrc:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Sanitizer Ci applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run expensive dynamic checks in a documented matrix with symbolized actionable output.
